fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp=False and skips it when clamp=True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, but `if not clamp` inverts this — the condition entering the branch should be `clamp`, not `not clamp`.
The clamping branch body should only be entered when clamp is False (no-clamp mode), but due to the inverted condition it is entered when clamp is True.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the final y value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the bug causes this to be violated.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before returning, if clamping was requested the result must be bounded by the segment's y range.
